front: What is the future roadmap for Apache Iceberg — multi-table transactions, encryption, and Iceberg v3?
back: Iceberg v3 (spec in development as of 2025) brings significant enhancements. Multi-table transactions: currently each Iceberg commit is atomic within a single table; v3 aims to support cross-table atomic commits via catalog-level transaction coordination — enabling 'transfer money from A to B' style atomic writes across multiple tables. Column-level encryption: encrypt specific sensitive columns (PII) independently of the rest of the row, with per-column key management — enabling compliance without encrypting the entire file. Deletion vectors: a more efficient delete file format (borrowing from Delta Lake's approach) that stores deleted row positions as bitsets, reducing merge overhead at read time. Improved partition statistics: more granular per-partition statistics to enable smarter query optimization. Row lineage tracking: built-in provenance tracking for data governance. On the ecosystem side: universal REST catalog adoption is accelerating, with major cloud providers (AWS S3Tables, Google BigLake, Snowflake) all implementing the spec. The trajectory: Iceberg is becoming the 'open table standard' for the data industry, similar to how Parquet became the standard file format.
